metrics.narrative: Budget was verbally confirmed at roughly $150K+ SaaS (equal or more for services) by Cory Griffiths around 2 Apr 2025, broadly consistent with the current $195,000 opportunity amount, but has never been revalidated since. Pain is framed in the CRM MEDDPICC record (synced 10 Oct 2024) as limited enterprise spend visibility and redundant vendors for the same commodity. During the 11 Sep 2025 demo (Avoma), a buyer-side speaker noted reviews and QA tasks were 'somewhat repetitive,' adding operational color but no new dollar figure.

sources: Known risk note re: Brian Lambert auto-reply, Jun 2026
champion.narrative: Rod Copple was the original named champion (CRM MEDDPICC, synced 10 Oct 2024) but ceded procurement ownership to Cory Griffiths in Q1 2025 and shows no direct engagement since. Cory Griffiths was 'receptive' on the 30 Jun 2025 reconnect call but 'has been quiet again' per that same Next Step entry, and by 26 Dec 2025 the rep's working contact had shifted to Brian Lambert, whose responsiveness is now in question given a stale January out-of-office auto-reply resurfacing in June 2026. No one on the buyer side is currently confirmed as actively advocating for Zycus internally.

competition.narrative: Salesforce's Competitors__c field names Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba and iCertis, and the 9 Jun 2025 Next Step entry additionally names DDSI as the incumbent P2P system LCS currently runs, with the buyer stating a P2P replacement was '2-3 years out.' The 11 Sep 2025 demo shows the buying team asking broad discovery questions about existing systems and ERP connections rather than comparing named rivals, and no source since names a leading alternative or an active bake-off.

identify_pain.narrative: The CRM MEDDPICC field (synced 10 Oct 2024) names limited enterprise spend visibility and redundant vendors for the same commodity as the core pain. The 2 Apr 2025 call confirmed the buyer's wish to consolidate onto one S2P platform instead of point solutions, with DDSI currently running P2P (per the 9 Jun 2025 Next Step entry). In the 11 Sep 2025 demo, a buyer-side speaker flagged repetitive review/QA tasks and another described needing to import externally-supplied member data into their tool, adding concrete operational detail.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifact (MSA, SOW, Order Form, redline) has surfaced in any source. The deal has not progressed past Formal Evaluation, and the buyer's own RFP - the document that would define the paper process to come - has still not been delivered as of this sweep (9 Jul 2026), roughly nine months after it was first promised.

economic_buyer.narrative: Dan Lahey, EVP & CFO, is named as economic buyer and budget owner in the CRM MEDDPICC record (synced 10 Oct 2024), and the decision-process field explicitly states Rod Copple 'will need to obtain the budget from the CFO,' corroborating Lahey's role as budget holder. However, no Salesforce task, Next Step entry, or Avoma call across the life of this opportunity shows any direct Zycus interaction with Lahey - all contact has run through Rod Copple and, since Q1 2025, Cory Griffiths. The role is confirmed by name but unengaged, which is a live risk to closing $195,000.

decision_process.narrative: Per the CRM MEDDPICC decision-process field (synced 10 Oct 2024), Rod Copple, a 28-year LCS veteran, recently took over procurement and reports directly to Dan Lahey, who holds budget authority. Per the Next_Step_History__c narrative, Rod handed day-to-day procurement ownership to Cory Griffiths in Q1 2025, and the evaluation has since run at the Griffiths/Lambert level via an internally-built RFP (still incomplete as of 16 Mar 2026) rather than the 'no RFP expected' path originally signaled in April 2025. The reporting line to the CFO is known, but no C-level checkpoint has been confirmed since the handoff.

decision_criteria.narrative: On the 2 Apr 2025 introductory call, Cory Griffiths conveyed a preference for a single full-suite Source-to-Pay platform spanning Procurement, Sourcing, Spend Analytics, Contracts, Supplier Management and Project & Savings Management, rather than stitching together point solutions. The 11 Sep 2025 demo walked the LCS/PCS team through each of those modules plus Merlin-powered autonomous sourcing and multi-ERP integration (Oracle, SAP, Dynamics, Salesforce), and buyer speakers asked detailed follow-up questions on data formats and ERP connectivity. A formal RFP meant to codify these criteria has been 'in creation' since at least 24 Oct 2025 and remains undelivered as of the 16 Mar 2026 Next Step update.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 08/28/2024; 708 days old. Close date 08/27/2026 is 51 days away. In Formal Evaluation stage continuously since creation (no stage progression in 23 months). Buyer stated '2-3 years out for P2P replacement' on 06/09/2025; that timeline places implementation in 2027-2028, far beyond the Aug 2026 close date. Last real buyer engagement: demo on 09/12/2025 (received well, awaiting shortlist decision), then RFP promises in Oct 2025, Dec 2025, and March 2026 — none delivered. Rep-initiated outreach on 06/30/2026 ('awaiting buyer reply'), but no two-way buyer engagement since June 2025 (13+ months). At current pace (no measurable progress in 10+ months, no RFP, no shortlist confirmation, no commercial conversation), deal cannot close in 51 days. No quantified ROI or business case to justify $195K investment to EB/CFO.

Re-anchor the relationship above the IT layer: with Brian Lambert's reachability in doubt, use Elisa Baptiste (SVP/COO, warmly engaged post-demo) and Cory Griffiths (original procurement lead) to keep multiple live threads into a 5-vendor evaluation.

Get the economic buyer, Dan Lahey, directly engaged: budget sign-off runs through him per the account's own MEDDPICC notes, yet no call or email has ever reached him, leaving the $195K Best Case forecast unvalidated.

Force a firm RFP delivery date: the RFP has slipped three times (Oct 2025, Dec 2025, Mar 2026) with no consequence; propose a mutual close plan with a hard date to convert the Aug 2026 close from aspirational to real.

best_practice_check.summary: The single highest-leverage move is rebuilding an active champion and securing indirect or direct economic-buyer visibility; the RFP delay is the immediate blocker to watch.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer speaker across the 2 Apr 2025 intro call or 11 Sep 2025 demo voices a preference for Zycus over Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba, iCertis, or continuing on DDSI; the only positive note is the rep's own comment that the demo was 'received well' (Next_Step__c, 12 Sep 2025), which is rep-reported, not buyer-voiced.

competitive_position.summary: Five named suite competitors sit in Salesforce with no dated evidence of current positioning against any of them, and the more immediate threat is the do-nothing path of staying on the incumbent DDSI system, which the buyer indicated in June 2025 may run another two to three years before replacement.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A deal with a genuinely good fit story (full-suite S2P preference confirmed and demoed well) that has stalled on process: the buyer's own RFP has slipped three promised dates, the economic buyer has never been directly engaged, and the original champion went silent after handing off ownership.

deal_risk.text: Five named competitors with zero fresh intel - Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba and iCertis sit in the Competitors__c field with no dated call evidence of current positioning against any of them.
Forecast category unsupported by process stage - the deal carries Best Case with a 49-day close, yet remains in Formal Evaluation awaiting a buyer RFP that has already slipped three times.

win_position.text: Economic buyer never engaged - CFO Dan Lahey is named as budget holder in the CRM MEDDPICC record (synced 10 Oct 2024) but no call, task, or Next Step across the opportunity's life shows direct Zycus contact with him.

Champion currently inactive - Rod Copple handed procurement ownership to Cory Griffiths in Q1 2025, Griffiths went quiet after being 'receptive' on 30 Jun 2025, and the most recent named contact (Brian Lambert, 26 Dec 2025) has a stale Jan out-of-office auto-reply resurfacing in Jun 2026.

Full-suite fit confirmed by the buyer - Cory Griffiths told Zycus on 2 Apr 2025 that LCS wants one S2P platform instead of point solutions, and the 11 Sep 2025 demo walked through every module the buyer named.
RFP overdue nine-plus months - LCS has promised a completed RFP since 24 Oct 2025, then Dec 2025, then again as of 16 Mar 2026, and it still has not arrived as of this sweep (9 Jul 2026).

deal_momentum.text: No verified two-way buyer contact in 241 days - the last confirmed buyer-side email reply was 10 Nov 2025; the rep's most recent outreach (30 Jun 2026) is still awaiting a reply.
Rep re-engaged nine days ago - Bailey Erazo reached back out to LCS on 30 Jun 2026 after the Next Step had sat at 'Awaiting RFP' since 16 Mar 2026.

Deal has repeatedly flirted with closeout - the Next Step trail shows 'likely closeout' language on 14 Jul 2025 and 'reaching out once more... if nothing, closing out' on 15 Aug 2025, both predating the current stall.

customer_commitment.text: Buyer's own stated timeline conflicts with the close date - on 9 Jun 2025 the buyer said P2P replacement was '2-3 years out,' well past the current 27 Aug 2026 close date (49 days from today).

Buyer engaged seriously in the past - Cory Griffiths and Elissa Rogers scheduled and held a reconnect call on 9 Jun 2025, and the 11 Sep 2025 demo drew a multi-person LCS/PCS team asking detailed integration and analytics questions.
